description: Policies API — Full CRUD for Issue Policies, PR Policies, Test Scheduling Policies, Policy Assignments V2, Policy Settings, Policy Actions
constraints: Follow existing 3-layer pattern: MCP Tool → Service → API
Use Zod schemas as plain objects (not z.object()), matching existing tools
All tools must be registered in src/mcp/tools/index.ts
Vendor media types required for all API calls (Accept + Content-Type headers)
deno task check && deno task lint && deno fmt --check must all pass
Maximum 5 rules per policy, 5 fixByRule entries per rule
nonGoals: Policy Evaluations and Violations (endpoints truncated in spec)
Portfolio Policy Configuration (endpoints not fully documented)

glossary.issue policy: Policy that triggers actions (email, break build, Jira ticket) when issues match severity/tool filters
glossary.PR policy: Policy that blocks pull requests when findings match severity/tool criteria
glossary.policy assignment: Link between a policy and a project — assigns the policy to that project
glossary.powerFilterQuery: RSQL-style query inside policy rules for matching issues (e.g., context:tool-type=in=('sast'))
glossary.criteriaQuery: RSQL-style query inside PR policy rules (same syntax as powerFilterQuery)

userStories.title: Add Policies types and API client
userStories.description: Add type definitions to src/types/polaris.ts for all policy-related types. Create src/api/policies.ts with endpoint functions. Create src/services/policies.ts with service wrappers. Export from src/services/index.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: src/types/polaris.ts exports: IssuePolicy, IssuePolicyPayload, PrPolicy, PrPolicyPayload, TestSchedulingPolicy, TestSchedulingPolicyPayload, PolicyAssignment, BulkAssignmentsPayload, PolicyAction, PolicySettingsPayload
IssuePolicy type includes: id, name, description, filterGroups (with rules array), isDefault, createdAt, updatedAt, _links
PrPolicy type includes: id, name, description, rules (with criteriaQuery and actions), createdAt, updatedAt, _links
TestSchedulingPolicy type includes: id, name, description, scheduleGroups (with rules array), isDefault, createdAt, updatedAt, _links
src/api/policies.ts creates all endpoint functions using the PolarisClient pattern from existing API modules

All API functions use correct vendor media types: application/vnd.polaris.policies.issue-policy-1+json, application/vnd.polaris.policies.pr-policies-1+json, application/vnd.polaris.policies.test-scheduling-policy-1+json, application/vnd.polaris.policies.policy-bulk-assignment-2+json, application/vnd.polaris.policies.action-1+json
src/services/policies.ts wraps API functions following existing service patterns
Services exported from src/services/index.ts
Typecheck passes: deno task check
userStories.notes: Completed - types, API client, and services implemented following existing patterns

hints: Follow the exact pattern from existing API modules (e.g., bug-tracking.ts) for the policies API client
The PolarisClient has methods like get(), post(), put(), delete() — use these
All collection endpoints return PaginatedResponse<T> with _items, _links, _collection
Base path for all endpoints: /api/policies/
Issue policies path: /api/policies/issue-policies
PR policies path: /api/policies/pr-policies
Test scheduling path: /api/policies/test-scheduling-policies
Assignments path: /api/policies/assignments
Actions path: /api/policies/actions
Policy settings path: /api/policies/policy-settings

userStories.title: Implement Issue Policies CRUD tools
userStories.description: Create MCP tools: get_issue_policies, get_issue_policy, create_issue_policy, update_issue_policy, delete_issue_policy.
acceptanceCriteria: get_issue_policies tool: schema has filter (optional), offset (optional), limit (optional), associationId (optional). Returns paginated list.
get_issue_policy tool: schema has policy_id (required). Returns single policy.

create_issue_policy tool: schema has name (required, max 255), description (optional, max 512), filter_groups with rules array. Each rule has ruleNumber, powerFilterQuery, actions array, optional fixByRule array.
update_issue_policy tool: schema has policy_id (required) plus same fields as create (full replacement).
delete_issue_policy tool: schema has policy_id (required). Returns 204.
All tools describe valid powerFilterQuery syntax: context:tool-type=in=('sast','sca','dast'), issueProperties:severity=in=('critical','high','medium','low')
All tools describe valid actions: SEND_EMAIL, BREAK_THE_BUILD, CREATE_BUNDLE_JIRA_TICKET
All tools registered in src/mcp/tools/index.ts
Typecheck passes: deno task check

hints: Follow the same CRUD pattern as bug-tracking-config tools
create_issue_policy returns 201, update returns 200, delete returns 204
Include rule constraints in tool descriptions: max 5 rules, max 5 fixByRule per rule
Put each tool in its own file following naming convention: get-issue-policies.ts, create-issue-policy.ts, etc.

userStories.title: Implement PR Policies and Test Scheduling Policies tools
userStories.description: Create MCP tools for PR policies (CRUD) and test scheduling policies (create, list, get, update — no delete).
acceptanceCriteria: get_pr_policies, get_pr_policy, create_pr_policy, update_pr_policy, delete_pr_policy tools created
PR policy tools describe rules with criteriaQuery (same syntax as powerFilterQuery) and actions (only BLOCK_PR)
get_test_scheduling_policies, get_test_scheduling_policy, create_test_scheduling_policy, update_test_scheduling_policy tools created
Test scheduling tools describe scheduleGroups with rules having ruleNumber and frequency (daily/weekly)
No delete tool for test scheduling policies (not supported by API)
All tools use correct vendor media types
All tools registered in src/mcp/tools/index.ts
Typecheck passes: deno task check

hints: PR policies use 'rules' directly (not wrapped in filterGroups like issue policies)
PR policy actions only support BLOCK_PR
Test scheduling policies have scheduleGroups.rules with ruleNumber and frequency
Frequency values: 'daily' or 'weekly' only
Multi-policy support is not available for test scheduling (one per project/branch)

userStories.title: Implement Policy Assignments V2 tools
userStories.description: Create MCP tools: get_policy_assignments, get_policy_assignment, create_policy_assignments (bulk), delete_policy_assignments (bulk).
acceptanceCriteria: get_policy_assignments tool: schema has filter (required — API requires it), offset, limit. Description notes filter is required (e.g., policyId=='uuid')
get_policy_assignment tool: schema has assignment_id (required). Returns single assignment.
create_policy_assignments tool: schema has assignments array of {type, associationId, policyId}. POST to /api/policies/assignments. Returns 204.
delete_policy_assignments tool: schema has same assignments array. DELETE to /api/policies/assignments. Returns 204.
All tools use V2 media type: application/vnd.polaris.policies.policy-bulk-assignment-2+json
Tool descriptions note: up to 5 issue policies per project, 1 test scheduling policy per project
All tools registered in src/mcp/tools/index.ts
Typecheck passes: deno task check

hints: create and delete both send an assignments array in the request body
create is POST, delete is DELETE — both return 204 No Content
GET requires a filter — document this clearly (e.g., policyId=='uuid' or associationId=='uuid')
type field is typically 'project'

userStories.title: Implement Policy Settings and Policy Actions tools
userStories.description: Create MCP tools: update_policy_settings (set default policy), get_policy_actions, get_policy_action.
acceptanceCriteria: update_policy_settings tool: schema has policy_id (required), default_policy_status (required boolean). POST to /api/policies/policy-settings. Returns 204.
Tool description notes: new default policy will NOT be reassigned to existing projects
get_policy_actions tool: schema has filter (optional, e.g., policyUseCase=='issue_policy'), offset, limit. Returns paginated list.
get_policy_action tool: schema has action_id (required). Returns single action.
Tool descriptions list supported actions: SEND_EMAIL, BREAK_THE_BUILD, CREATE_BUNDLE_JIRA_TICKET, BLOCK_PR
All tools registered in src/mcp/tools/index.ts
Typecheck passes: deno task check
deno task lint passes
deno fmt --check passes

hints: Policy settings uses Content-Type: application/json (not vendor type)
Policy actions are read-only — only GET endpoints
Actions can be filtered by policyUseCase: 'issue_policy' or 'component_policy'
update_policy_settings is actually a POST (not PUT/PATCH)
